title: CypherX Step-Up Approval Token Claims (Contract 16)
description: 📋 Defined now for alignment; ENFORCEMENT TURNS ON IN PHASE 2 (see Phase 2 Component 10 for the full grant flow — this contract pins only the wire format and verification rules). Claims of the Approval Token presented in the X-Approval-Token header alongside the agent JWT when an agent invokes an approval-required scope (e.g. payments:execute, data:bulk_delete, infra:write, external_api:write, agent:create_subagent, policy:write). Forward-compatibility: additionalProperties is true and only validation-mandatory claims are required. TTL (exp - iat) MUST be ≤ 15 min for one-shot and ≤ 1 hour for multi-shot tokens.

iss.description: Issuer. Resolves to the deployment's AUTH_ISSUER_URL (e.g. https://auth.cypherx.ai).

sub.description: Subject — the agent UUID the approval is bound to.

aud.description: Audience. MUST contain the configured AUTH_PLATFORM_AUDIENCE of the verifying deployment.

exp.description: Expiry (NumericDate). MUST be in the future. exp - iat MUST be ≤ 900s (one-shot) or ≤ 3600s (multi-shot).

tenant_id.description: Org UUID. MUST match the tenant_id of the bound agent JWT.

agent_id.description: Agent UUID. MUST match the agent_id of the bound agent JWT.

grant_id.description: References auth.approval_grants.grant_id. For one-shot tokens, the row is atomically consumed at verification (consumed_at), else APPROVAL_EXHAUSTED.

approved_by.description: px0 user UUID of the approver. MUST be a different user identity from the agent's owner when the owner is a human user (prevents self-approval on hijacked credentials).

approval_scopes.description: Approval-required scope(s) this token grants (e.g. ["payments:execute"]). Verification requires this to contain the scope needed for the action.

approval_resource.description: Resource the approval covers (e.g. payment:invoice-123). MAY be "*" for multi-shot tokens within a workflow scope; for one-shot tokens it MUST be a concrete resource identifier. Verification requires it to match the resource being acted on (or be "*").

approval_task.description: Binds the token to a specific xAgent/Workflow task (matched against header X-Task-ID). A token cannot be reused on a different task.

step_up_method.description: The step-up authentication method satisfied by the approver. Must satisfy tenant policy at verification time.

one_shot.description: Default true. When true the grant is atomically consumed on first use (≤ 15 min TTL, concrete approval_resource). Multi-shot (false) requires explicit grant-time opt-in and a documented business reason (≤ 1 hour TTL).
